title: Regex: движок, RegexOptions.Compiled и GeneratedRegex
note: The sandbox references + imports System.Text.RegularExpressions, so each card runs the SAME natural new Regex(...) / RegexOptions.Compiled code shown to the learner (no reflection): shown == executed. [GeneratedRegex] source generators run at project compile time, not in CSharpScript, so they are taught conceptually and the runtime behavior is proven with new Regex / RegexOptions.Compiled.

cards.prompt: new Regex(@"\d{3}-\d{4}").IsMatch on "call 555-1234 now" is True, and Match(...).Value returns only the matched substring 555-1234, printing True then 555-1234
cards.code: using System; using System.Text.RegularExpressions; var re = new Regex(@"\d{3}-\d{4}"); Console.WriteLine(re.IsMatch("call 555-1234 now")); Console.WriteLine(re.Match("call 555-1234 now").Value);

cards.prompt: new Regex(@"\w+", RegexOptions.Compiled).Matches("one two three") finds 3 matches and ms[2].Value is three; RegexOptions.Compiled changes the engine (IL via reflection-emit) not the result, printing 3 then three
cards.code: using System; using System.Text.RegularExpressions; var re = new Regex(@"\w+", RegexOptions.Compiled); var ms = re.Matches("one two three"); Console.WriteLine(ms.Count); Console.WriteLine(ms[2].Value);

cards.prompt: new Regex(@"(\w+)@(\w+)", RegexOptions.IgnoreCase) matched against "USER@Example rest" captures Groups[1]=USER and Groups[2]=Example, and Replace("a@b c@d", "X") replaces each match giving X X, printing USER, Example, X X
cards.code: using System; using System.Text.RegularExpressions; var re = new Regex(@"(\w+)@(\w+)", RegexOptions.IgnoreCase); var m = re.Match("USER@Example rest"); Console.WriteLine(m.Groups[1].Value); Console.WriteLine(m.Groups[2].Value); Console.WriteLine(re.Replace("a@b c@d", "X"));
